rationale.R1.1: The section opens with three numbered contribution claims; a review must assess whether each claim is accurately and consistently stated — e.g., whether 'learned router' and 'novel uncertainty calibration procedure' actually describe the methods that follow.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: multiple internal contradictions are present. Contribution (1) asserts a 'learned router' but the method is a fixed scalar threshold τ chosen by ROC sweep — no learning occurs. Contribution (2) asserts a 'novel uncertainty calibration procedure based on pathology co-occurrence priors' but no such procedure appears in the methods or results section; the final paragraph then defers co-occurrence priors to future work, directly contradicting its status as a delivered contribution. These contradictions invalidate the section's core claims if unresolved.
rationale.R1.3: Central: specific empirical claims (31% escalation rate, AUROC 0.889 ±0.004, latency reduction 4.2→1.6 s, five-pathology scope) must align with reported experiments; contribution (2) has no corresponding quantitative result anywhere in the section, creating an unresolved evidence gap.
